info.title: Features Service API
info.description:
Manages feature definitions for the dashboard — inputs, outputs, charts, and metadata.

## Key Concepts

**Features** define what campaigns can do: their input form, output metrics, charts, and workflow columns. Each feature has a unique slug (e.g. `outlet-database-discovery`) auto-generated from its name.

**Inputs** define the fields shown in the campaign creation form. Each input has an `extractKey` that maps to brand-service's extract-fields API, enabling AI-powered pre-fill from brand data.

**Pre-fill flow** (for LLMs and workflows):
1. Call `GET /features/{slug}/inputs` to see what fields a feature needs and their extractKeys
2. Call `POST /features/{slug}/prefill` with a brandId to get AI-extracted values keyed by input key
3. Pass the prefilled values to downstream services (e.g. outlets-service discover endpoint)

The prefill endpoint returns **named keys** (not positional arrays), so workflows can reference fields by name (e.g. `$ref:prefill.output.prefilled.industry`) instead of fragile index-based mapping.

## Available Features

| Slug | Category | Channel | Description |
|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `outlet-database-discovery` | pr | database | Discover relevant press outlets via AI search + scoring |
| *(more via GET /features)* | | | |

slug.description: Unique machine-readable identifier, auto-generated from name (e.g. 'outlet-database-discovery')

signature.description: Deterministic hash of sorted input+output keys — used for idempotent upsert

inputs.description: Input fields for the campaign creation form. Each input has an extractKey that maps to brand-service's extract-fields API for AI pre-fill.

outputs.description: Output metrics displayed on the dashboard

workflowColumns.description: Column definitions for the workflow performance table

resultComponent.description: Specialized result component slug (e.g. 'discovered-outlets'), null for standard features

defaultWorkflowName.description: Default workflow name in workflow-service, null if no default

brandId.description: Brand that was used for pre-fill

prefilled.description: Map of input key → flattened text value (or null if extraction failed). Keys match the feature's inputs[].key. Example: { industry: 'Enterprise SaaS', angles: 'Series B funding, AI product launch', targetGeo: 'US and UK' }

PrefillTextResponse.description: Pre-filled values as flat strings, ready for form inputs or workflow inputMapping

brandId.description: Brand that was used for pre-fill

value.description: Extracted value — can be a string, array, or object depending on the field

cached.description: Whether the value was served from cache (brand-service caches extractions for 30 days)

sourceUrls.description: URLs from the brand's website that were used to extract this value

prefilled.description: Map of input key → full extraction result. Keys match the feature's inputs[].key. Example: { industry: { value: 'Enterprise SaaS', cached: true, sourceUrls: ['https://example.com/about'] } }

PrefillFullResponse.description: Pre-filled values with metadata (cache status, source URLs)

inputs.description: Input definitions. Each input's extractKey maps to a brand-service extract-fields key. Use these extractKeys when calling POST /brands/{brandId}/extract-fields, or call POST /features/{slug}/prefill to let the service handle extraction automatically.

put.description: Idempotent — safe to call on every cold start. Uses a signature (hash of sorted input+output keys) to detect duplicates: same signature → upsert metadata, same name but different signature → auto-suffix with v2, v3, etc.

get.summary: Get a single feature by slug
get.description: Returns the full feature definition including inputs, outputs, charts, and workflow columns. The inputs array contains extractKey fields that map to brand-service for AI-powered pre-fill.

put.summary: Update a single feature by slug
put.description: Partial update — only provided fields are changed. If name changes, slug is re-generated.

get.summary: Get input definitions for a feature
get.description:
Returns the input schema for a feature. Each input has:
- **key**: machine-readable identifier used in prefill responses and workflow inputMapping
- **extractKey**: the key passed to brand-service's POST /brands/{brandId}/extract-fields for AI extraction
- **description**: rich context used by the LLM during extraction — describes what a good value looks like

**Workflow integration**: Use these inputs to build extract-fields requests, or call POST /features/{slug}/prefill which handles extraction automatically and returns values keyed by input key.

**Example** for 'outlet-database-discovery':
```json
{
  "slug": "outlet-database-discovery",
  "name": "Outlet Database Discovery",
  "inputs": [
    { "key": "industry", "extractKey": "industry", "type": "text", ... },
    { "key": "angles", "extractKey": "suggestedAngles", "type": "text", ... },
    { "key": "targetGeo", "extractKey": "suggestedGeo", "type": "text", ... }
  ]
}
```

inputs.description: Input definitions. Each input's extractKey maps to a brand-service extract-fields key. Use these extractKeys when calling POST /brands/{brandId}/extract-fields, or call POST /features/{slug}/prefill to let the service handle extraction automatically.

post.summary: Pre-fill input values for a feature from brand data
post.description:
Takes a brandId, looks up the feature's inputs, calls brand-service to extract values via AI (cached 30 days per field), and returns pre-filled values mapped to each input key.

**How it works internally**:
1. Reads the feature's inputs[].extractKey to build the extract-fields request
2. Calls brand-service POST /brands/{brandId}/extract-fields with those keys
3. Maps results back to input keys and returns a named object (not a positional array)

**format=text** (recommended for workflows): Returns `{ prefilled: { industry: 'SaaS', angles: 'Series B funding', targetGeo: 'US' } }` — flat string values ready to pass directly as workflow inputs or form values.

**format=full** (default): Returns `{ prefilled: { industry: { value: 'SaaS', cached: true, sourceUrls: [...] } } }` — includes cache status and source URLs for UI display.

**Workflow pattern**: In a workflow DAG, use this endpoint instead of calling brand-service extract-fields directly. This ensures the extracted fields always match what the feature expects, with no index-based mapping fragility:
```
prefill-node → downstream-service
  inputMapping: { body.industry: '$ref:prefill-node.output.prefilled.industry' }
```

schema.description: Response format: 'text' for flat strings (best for workflows), 'full' for values + metadata (default)

parameters.description: Response format: 'text' for flat strings (best for workflows), 'full' for values + metadata (default)
parameters.name: format

200.description: Pre-filled values. Response shape depends on ?format= query param.

brandId.description: Brand that was used for pre-fill

prefilled.description: Map of input key → flattened text value (or null if extraction failed). Keys match the feature's inputs[].key. Example: { industry: 'Enterprise SaaS', angles: 'Series B funding, AI product launch', targetGeo: 'US and UK' }

oneOf.description: Pre-filled values as flat strings, ready for form inputs or workflow inputMapping

value.description: Extracted value — can be a string, array, or object depending on the field

cached.description: Whether the value was served from cache (brand-service caches extractions for 30 days)

sourceUrls.description: URLs from the brand's website that were used to extract this value

prefilled.description: Map of input key → full extraction result. Keys match the feature's inputs[].key. Example: { industry: { value: 'Enterprise SaaS', cached: true, sourceUrls: ['https://example.com/about'] } }

oneOf.description: Pre-filled values with metadata (cache status, source URLs)
